research_findings: The new secretariat fields are durable after v43, but later operators still cannot see them in project snapshots or version reports.
The next smallest step is visibility: summarize secretary output in collect-project-data and write-report without changing gates.
Keeping the rendered output compact preserves the lightweight committee model while improving handoff quality.
committee_feedback: Product Council: the convergence layer only helps operators if they can actually see it in reports and snapshots.
Architecture Council: render only compact secretary summaries and decision records, not the full capture payload.
Operator Council: visibility should improve handoff quality without making reports noisy.

research_gate.summary: Expose compact delivery and audit secretary summaries in project-data snapshots and reports.

product_council.summary: Make the secretariat visible so later sessions can understand how council feedback became an executable decision.
product_council.decision: Approve snapshot and report rendering only; keep readiness behavior unchanged.

architecture_council.summary: Read the new secretariat fields from review_state and surface a narrow summary in collect-project-data and write-report.
architecture_council.decision: Prefer summary strings and open-gap counts over duplicating full secretary payloads.

operator_council.summary: A later maintainer should be able to scan the report and snapshot and understand the secretary outcome quickly.
operator_council.decision: Approve if the output remains concise and clearly separated from committee and scope sections.

delivery_secretary.summary: The delivery and audit outputs now exist durably, so the next step is to expose them where later operators already look.
delivery_secretary.next_action: Render compact secretariat summaries in project snapshots and version reports.

audit_secretary.summary: Visibility should focus on secretary summaries, decision records, and open gaps, not full raw capture payloads.
audit_secretary.decision_record: The convergence layer becomes more useful once reports and snapshots surface it directly for handoff.
open_gaps: Readiness and gate decisions still do not consume secretariat output directly.

scope_decision.selected_goal: Expose secretariat output in project-data snapshots and reports
scope_decision.why_selected: The new secretariat fields are durable, but still effectively hidden from operators unless they inspect state.json directly.
scope_in: Add a compact secretariat summary to latest_review_state in collected project data.
Render delivery and audit secretary output in version reports.
Extend validation to cover the new report and snapshot visibility.
scope_out: Do not change readiness or publication gates.
Do not add more secretariat fields.
stop_conditions: Stop if secretariat visibility duplicates full state blobs instead of concise summaries.
dissent: Keep secretary visibility compact; the report should not become a dump of every captured field.
scope_decision.next_action: Use the now-visible convergence layer to improve evaluator ergonomics in the next version.

critique: This turns the secretariat from hidden state into a visible handoff tool while keeping the model lightweight.
minimum_fixes_required: Keep the rendered secretary output concise and clearly separated from committee summaries.

review_state.goal_title: Expose secretariat output in project-data snapshots and reports

Integrate data snapshot and quality state into the loop's durable state model

Improve next-goal selection using data quality gaps and evidence gaps
